title: Expressing support for the designation of February 9, 2025, as the first ever "Gulf of America Day" and celebrating the importance of changing the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America.
plain_summary: This bill supports making February 9, 2025 the first ever "Gulf of America Day." It celebrates President Trump's decision to change the name of the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America. The Gulf of America is the large body of water that touches five southern states. The bill shows Congress supports this name change and wants to have a special day to recognize it.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution supports making February ninth, two thousand twenty-five the first ever Gulf of America Day. It also celebrates the importance of changing the name from Gulf of Mexico to Gulf of America.

sections.title: Background Information
sections.text: On January twentieth, two thousand twenty-five, President Trump signed an executive order called Restoring Names That Honor American Greatness. This order told the Secretary of the Interior to rename the water area as the Gulf of America. This area is surrounded by the states of Texas, Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama, and Florida. It extends out to the border with Mexico and Cuba in the water. This area used to be called the Gulf of Mexico. The Gulf has always been an important part of America and helped the nation grow. February ninth, two thousand twenty-five marks President Trump's first visit to the Gulf after it was renamed. As President Trump works to restore American pride in the country's great history, it makes sense for the nation to come together and celebrate this important event and the renaming of the Gulf of America.

sections.title: House Resolution
sections.text: The House of Representatives does two things. First, it supports making the first ever Gulf of America Day. Second, it celebrates the importance of changing the name from Gulf of Mexico to Gulf of America.
